question.text: Rajiv has a total income of Rs. 18,00,000 during FY 2025-26 under the new tax regime. What is his income tax liability before surcharge and cess?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the new regime FY 2025-26 slabs applied on total income of Rs. 18,00,000: Up to 4L = Nil; 4-8L = 4L × 5% = Rs. 20,000; 8-12L = 4L × 10% = Rs. 40,000; 12-16L = 4L × 15% = Rs. 60,000; 16-18L = 2L × 20% = Rs. 40,000. Total tax = 20,000 + 40,000 + 60,000 + 40,000 = Rs. 1,60,000. **The Trap:** Rs. 2,40,000 results from applying old regime logic (30% flat on excess above 10L). Rs. 1,70,000 comes from mistakenly applying 25% instead of 20% on the 16-18L slab. Rs. 1,50,000 omits one slab layer. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A MFD exam also tests awareness of tax regimes for recommending tax-saving mutual funds. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always use the exact slab table for the specific FY — the new regime slabs changed significantly between FY 2024-25 and FY 2025-26.
